what_it_does: A posted note by Tim that comments on item-7ad77ae2, arguing that binary media already have an addressing scheme (blob://) so future vision support mainly needs a reader/viewer, while warning that most current address systems are provisional footholds that will need to be redone.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies this as a posted note with id item-c22b5853 and board:// URI address
Authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel with no thread
Links to item-7ad77ae2 via a commented_on relationship
Created and updated timestamps are identical
Body begins with [point] and contains two ideas: agreement with the blob:// addressing argument, and a caveat that many address systems are temporary
standouts: blob:// already provides addressing for images/audio, so vision needs a 'seeing reader' rather than new storage
Most current address systems are described as incorrect footholds that will need redoing
The note frames existing work as scaffolding produced by the author's development constraints
conventions: Follows the noticeboard markdown format with YAML frontmatter and a markdown body
Uses board:// URI for the item address
Uses a [point] marker in the body to label the note's role
Includes history array with a filed transition
notes: Tim's note says binary media addressing is already solved by blob://, so vision work should focus on a reader, but warns that most current address systems are provisional and will be rebuilt.
questions: What does 'vision' refer to in this context?
Which specific address systems are considered incorrect or temporary?
What is the scope and purpose of the dragnet-development channel?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose as a point/comment matches the actual content.
apparent_themes: separation of storage addressing from interpretation
intention_signals: It appears to steer design toward reusing existing blob:// addressing for visual/binary media and away from creating new storage/address layers, while managing expectations that much of the current addressing will be refactored.
novelty: The framing that the missing piece for vision is a 'seeing reader' rather than a place to put what it sees, plus the explicit 'footholds' metaphor for provisional systems.
contribution: Records a design stance that binary media can reuse blob:// addressing, that vision support should prioritize interpretation/reading, and that current address systems should be treated as temporary scaffolding likely to be rebuilt.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting that binary media already use blob:// addressing, so future vision support needs a reader rather than new storage, and warning that most current address systems are provisional footholds.
intention_for_embedding: To steer the design of vision and media handling toward reusing existing blob addressing and to flag that current addressing schemes are likely temporary.
